slots.rod: RIVERTEX Fenix 11'5" (Match Rod, 6.5-17.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium protects against Northern Pike if one strikes. Bloodworms covers several panfish and small species. Hook #4 is properly sized for panfish.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-7 ft near shore structure. At Woods of the Eastern Bank, try 4-5 ft near weed edges.

issues: Bloodworms only covers Yellow Perch, Bluntnose Minnow, and Green Sunfish at Saint-Croix — limited species coverage
Missing Pumpkinseed (needs Semolina Balls/Dough Balls/Red Worms/Maggots), Rock Bass (needs Night Crawlers/Leeches)
Titanium .004" (7 Lb) is light — upgrade to .005" for pike protection
improvements: Switch to Red Worms or Maggots for broader coverage — Maggots covers Pumpkinseed + Yellow Perch + panfish
Or switch to Night Crawlers to cover Rock Bass ($120/kg) + Yellow Perch + White Bass + Lake Sturgeon + Freshwater Drum
Deploy on RodPod Trio at shore for passive panfish while boating with active rods

slots.rod: SpookySpin 8'6" (Spinning Rod, Heavy, 5/7-1 3/4 Oz, 6.6-19.8 Lb, Mod Fast)
issues: Heavy rod handles Northern Pike (48.5 lb) but Muskie (65 lb) and Clear Muskie (69.5 lb) will severely test 19.8 Lb max line
Bullet Spinner's flash and vibration is excellent for pike and muskie in clear lake water
Titanium leader essential — ALL three muskie species and pike have razor teeth
improvements: Excellent predator rod for trolling from the Draco Bass Boat
Northern Pike ($170/kg, max 48.5 lb) is the TOP earner — up to $3,738 per trophy!
Walleye ($190/kg, max 23.2 lb) is highest $/kg — Bullet Spinner can trigger strikes near structure
If a Muskie (65-69.5 lb) hits, set drag very loose — the 12 Lb fluoro is your limit
Troll at moderate speed along weed edges and rocky points from the boat

slots.rod: JigWinner 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast)
issues: Light rod is perfect for Walleye ($190/kg), Smallmouth Bass ($160/kg), and trout species
If a pike or muskie hits this light setup, it will be a very challenging fight
Titanium .005" protects against bite-offs
improvements: Light jig is excellent for Walleye — they prefer slow, subtle presentations near bottom
Walleye at $190/kg (max 23.2 lb = up to $1,998 per trophy!) is the highest $/kg fish at Saint-Croix
Also targets Smallmouth Bass ($160/kg), Tiger Trout ($155/kg), Brown Trout ($150/kg), Brook Trout ($150/kg)
Cast from the Draco near rocky points and drop-offs — let jig sink and hop along bottom
Dawn and dusk are peak Walleye feeding times — focus this rod during those windows

slots.rod: Heavy Chaser 9'10" SE (Bottom Rod, X-Heavy, Casting 6-8.5 Oz, up to 81 Lb)
slots.terminal_tackle: Sinker 7-8 Oz, Catfish Hook #6/0, Simple Bell
issues: None for this location — 79 Lb line handles Blue Catfish (91.5 lb) at manageable tension
Lake Sturgeon (48.5 lb) is well within range
Shiners cover Blue Catfish, Muskie/Clear Muskie/Tiger Muskie, and Northern Pike
improvements: MONSTER BOTTOM RIG — perfectly matched for Saint-Croix's biggest fish
Blue Catfish (91.5 lb!) at ~86% tension on 79 Lb line — tough but landable with patience
Lake Sturgeon (48.5 lb) at comfortable 61% tension — needs Small Cutbait, Medium Cutbait, Shrimp, Crawfish Cut, or Night Crawlers for best results
Shiners also attracts all 3 Muskie species and Northern Pike if they're near bottom
Deploy on RodPod Trio at Sandy Cape or King of the Green Bay — deepest water access
NIGHT SESSIONS: Blue Catfish and Brown Bullhead become very active. Keep this soaking overnight.
Consider swapping bait to Large Cutbait or Night Crawlers to add Lake Sturgeon coverage — Shiners doesn't target Sturgeon

slots.rod: Rivertex Zeus 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, X-Heavy, 3/4-2 1/8 Oz, 18-46 Lb, Mod Fast)
issues: Clear Muskie at 69.5 lb will push the 41.2 Lb braid hard — ~70% tension
Blue Catfish at 91.5 lb exceeds line capacity — break-off risk on trophy blues
Heavy jig is good for deep structure where big predators hold
improvements: YOUR ACTIVE MUSKIE HUNTER — Zeus is the strongest spinning rod for targeting the 3 muskie species
Muskie ($100/kg, max 65 lb) and Clear Muskie ($100/kg, max 69.5 lb) are iconic catches
Northern Pike ($170/kg, max 48.5 lb = up to $3,738 per trophy!) — Zeus handles this comfortably
Troll heavy jig from the Draco along deep weed edges and rocky humps
Muskie are the 'fish of 10,000 casts' — be patient, they're rare but massive
Tiger Muskie ($120/kg, max 33.1 lb) is the most common of the three — good warm-up target

issues: Flies only covers Brook Trout at Saint-Croix — extremely limited at a 22-species location
Hook #1/0 is oversized for Brook Trout (max 4.0 lb) and panfish
No titanium leader — pike will snap this instantly
This slot is wasted potential at Saint-Croix
improvements: Switch bait to Semolina Balls — covers Pumpkinseed, Golden Shiner, and Green Sunfish for species completion
Or switch to Night Crawlers — covers Rock Bass ($120/kg), Yellow Perch, White Bass, Lake Sturgeon, and Freshwater Drum (5 species!)
Downsize hook to #4-#6 for panfish
Add titanium leader for pike protection
Deploy on RodPod Trio alongside slots 1 and 4 for passive shore fishing

issues: L'Impulsion reel is shared with slot 5 — can only use one at a time
83.8 Lb line handles everything at Saint-Croix including Blue Catfish (91.5 lb) at ~92% tension
improvements: ULTIMATE MONSTER FLOAT RIG — 83.8 Lb line on a float is the heaviest passive setup possible
Blue Catfish (91.5 lb) is your biggest target here — this is the only float rig that has a chance
Clear Muskie (69.5 lb) at ~83% tension — a real fight but landable
Northern Pike (48.5 lb) at comfortable ~58% tension
Lake Sturgeon (48.5 lb) — Shiners isn't the wiki bait but sturgeon are opportunistic
Deploy at Sandy Cape or King of the Green Bay — deep water with access to the biggest fish
Note: Swap L'Impulsion between slot 5 (active Zeus jig) and slot 7 (passive float) between sessions

gear.boat: Garry Scott Draco Bass Boat — USABLE! Boat fishing allowed at Saint-Croix
notes: Draco Bass Boat is your biggest advantage — use sonar to find muskie, pike, and walleye structure
Luminox Headlamp for night Blue Catfish sessions — they're most active after dark
RodPod Trio — deploy shore rods (slots 1, 4, 7) then head out on the Draco with slots 2, 3, 5
FishStronghold M Plus — fill with Northern Pike ($170/kg) and Walleye ($190/kg) for maximum income

groundbait.status: MyFeeder recipe — Catfish Destroyer + BigBeam Spicy + Oilcake-Pele
notes: Catfish Destroyer works for Blue Catfish bottom rig (slot 4)
Not used with the Heavy Chaser (bottom rod uses sinker, not feeder), but could attract catfish via scent if deployed nearby
Consider switching to a generic fish feeder base for broader species attraction at shore spots

boat.license_required: Advanced Michigan License ($8,000/day, $22,800/3 days, $50,400/week, 600 BC unlimited) — required for boat + night fishing

recommendation.speed: Bass boat with motor and sonar
recommendation.features: 24.4 ft aluminium bass boat with advanced sonar, rod holders, and trolling motor. Essential for reaching mid-lake muskie and pike structure.
recommendation.why: Saint-Croix Lake is large and deep — muskie, pike, and walleye hold on mid-lake structure that's inaccessible from shore. The Draco's sonar lets you find the underwater humps, weed edges, and rocky points where trophy fish lurk.

fishing_tips: Use sonar to locate deep structure — muskie and pike hold near underwater humps and weed edges
Troll slot 5 (Zeus + heavy jig) and slot 2 (SpookySpin + Bullet Spinner) along structure at slow speed
Cast slot 3 (JigWinner + light jig) toward rocky points for Walleye ($190/kg) at dawn/dusk
Muskie are rare — 'fish of 10,000 casts' — but three species (Muskie, Clear Muskie, Tiger Muskie) increases your odds
Northern Pike ($170/kg) are more common and nearly as valuable — target weed edges from the boat
Set up RodPod Trio at shore before heading out — slots 1 (panfish), 4 (Heavy Chaser bottom rig), and 7 (EquatorMatch heavy float)
Return to shore every 20-30 min to check passive rods and sell keepnet
Night from the boat: Drift over deep structure with slot 3 (light jig) for night-feeding Walleye
boat.cost_analysis: Advanced License $8,000/day for boat + night. Travel $7,800 + $2,000/day extend. Total 3-day trip: ~$30,400. One trophy Northern Pike ($170/kg x 48.5 lb = $3,738) + one trophy Walleye ($190/kg x 23.2 lb = $1,998) pays for the trip.

passive_fishing.best_stand_spot: Sandy Cape or King of the Green Bay
passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Deep water access from shore for monster bottom rig (slot 4). Sandy bottom at Sandy Cape is ideal for Blue Catfish and Lake Sturgeon. King of the Green Bay has rocky structure nearby for muskie/pike on the float rig (slot 7).
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Use the Draco to cover mid-lake structure. Troll deep weed edges with slots 2 and 5. Cast slot 3 at rocky points and humps for Walleye. Cover all 6 spots for discovery XP.
strategy: Deploy RodPod Trio at Sandy Cape or King of the Green Bay with slots 1, 4, and 7
Slot 4 (Heavy Chaser + 79 Lb + Shiners + sinker): Cast to deepest water — Blue Catfish (91.5 lb!) and bottom predators
Slot 7 (EquatorMatch + 83.8 Lb + Shiners + float): Cast to 8-12 ft — muskie and pike cruising mid-depth
Slot 1 (Fenix + Bloodworms + #4): Near shore at 3-5 ft — panfish species completion
Head out on Draco with slots 2, 3, and 5 — three active rods covering all predator depths
Troll slot 5 (Zeus + heavy jig) over deep structure for Muskie and Clear Muskie
Troll slot 2 (SpookySpin + Bullet Spinner) along weed edges for Northern Pike ($170/kg)
Cast slot 3 (JigWinner + light jig) at rocky points — WALLEYE ($190/kg) at dawn and dusk
Visit all 6 spots for location discovery XP: Fairytale Fishing, From Dawn Till Dusk, King of the Green Bay, Sandy Cape, Vanishing Rock Light, Woods of the Eastern Bank

NIGHT STRATEGY: Blue Catfish become very active. Return to shore, deploy all rods at the stand, and focus on the deep water with slots 4 and 7. Walleye also feeds actively at night — jig slot 3 from the boat.
income_estimate: Northern Pike ($170/kg, max 48.5 lb) — up to $3,738 per trophy! Best total income per fish.
Walleye ($190/kg, max 23.2 lb) — up to $1,998 per trophy. Highest $/kg at Saint-Croix.
Smallmouth Bass ($160/kg, max 11.9 lb) — up to $862 per trophy. Good active fishing target.
Tiger Trout ($155/kg, max 6.6 lb) — up to $463 per trophy. Nice bonus catches.
Brown Trout ($150/kg, max 15.7 lb) — up to $1,067 per trophy. Trolling target.
Tiger Muskie ($120/kg, max 33.1 lb) — up to $1,800 per trophy. Most common muskie.
Muskie/Clear Muskie ($100/kg, max 65-69.5 lb) — up to $3,150 per trophy! Rare but massive.
Blue Catfish ($70/kg, max 91.5 lb) — up to $2,903 per trophy. Night bottom rig.
Passive income: $500-$2,000/hr from shore rods (catfish + muskie/pike hits)
Active trolling: $2,000-$5,000/hr targeting pike + walleye + muskie from the Draco
Combined: $2,500-$7,000 per in-game hour. HIGHEST INCOME POTENTIAL OF ANY LOCATION.

missing_setups: Lake Sturgeon needs Small Cutbait, Medium Cutbait, Shrimp, Crawfish Cut, or Night Crawlers — swap slot 4 bait to Night Crawlers or bring Crawfish Cut
Rock Bass ($120/kg) needs Night Crawlers or Leeches — bring Night Crawlers on a float rod
Pumpkinseed needs Dough Balls, Semolina Balls, Red Worms, or Maggots — swap slot 1 or 6 bait
Golden Shiner needs Dough Balls or Semolina Balls — brief bait swap for completion
White Bass needs Night Crawlers or Small Minnows — covered if you bring Night Crawlers
xp_optimization: 22 species available — the most species-rich lake! Catch ALL for massive first-catch XP bonuses.
Unique Walleye, Northern Pike, Smallmouth Bass, Muskie, Clear Muskie, Tiger Muskie, Blue Catfish, and 5 more — 12 unique species!
Three types of Muskie at one location is RARE — catch all three for bragging rights + huge XP
Fish all 6 spots for location discovery XP
Night fishing unlocks Blue Catfish and Walleye peak activity — massive XP from trophies
Use the Draco boat to access mid-lake muskie structure — they rarely come close to shore
income_optimization: Walleye at $190/kg is the highest $/kg — target with light jig (slot 3) at dawn/dusk near rocky points
Northern Pike at $170/kg (max 48.5 lb = $3,738 per trophy!) — best total $/fish. Troll with Bullet Spinner.
Smallmouth Bass at $160/kg — solid income between muskie casts
Tiger Trout at $155/kg and Brown Trout at $150/kg — bonus income while trolling
Fill keepnet with Walleye + Northern Pike for highest average $/kg
Saint-Croix is expensive ($7,800 travel + $2,000/day + $8,000/day advanced license) but the income potential is the highest of any location you've visited
priority_changes: Switch slot 1 bait to Night Crawlers — covers Rock Bass ($120/kg), Yellow Perch, White Bass, Lake Sturgeon, and Freshwater Drum (5 species!)
Switch slot 6 bait from Flies to Semolina Balls — covers Pumpkinseed and Golden Shiner for species completion
Consider swapping slot 4 bait from Shiners to Night Crawlers or Crawfish Cut to add Lake Sturgeon coverage
Downsize slot 6 hook from #1/0 to #4-#6 and add titanium leader for pike protection
Focus on Walleye ($190/kg) at dawn/dusk and Northern Pike ($170/kg) during the day for maximum income
Night sessions: Blue Catfish with Heavy Chaser (slot 4) + Walleye with light jig from the boat (slot 3)
